steps.title: Simplify the ratio and identify the constraint
steps.content:
The ratio of length to width is 35 to 10. Simplify by dividing both by 5:
$$\frac{35}{10} = \frac{7}{2}$$
So the length-to-width ratio is 7:2. When the width increases, to maintain this ratio, the changes in length and width must themselves be in the same 7:2 ratio.

steps.title: Apply the ratio to the change in width
steps.content:
The width increases by 7 units. Call the length increase $\Delta L$. For the ratio to remain 7:2, the changes must satisfy:
$$\frac{\Delta L}{\Delta W} = \frac{7}{2}$$
Substitute $\Delta W = 7$:
$$\frac{\Delta L}{7} = \frac{7}{2}$$
$$\Delta L = 7 \cdot \frac{7}{2} = \frac{49}{2} = 24.5$$
Since $\Delta L = 24.5 > 0$, the length must increase by 24.5 units. That matches choice B — B is correct.
